researchTopic: Effects of Street Façade Design Strategies on Pedestrian Psychological Responses in Urban Renewal--A Neuro-Architectural Study Using AIGC-fNIRS Framework
organization: Effects of Street Façade Design Strategies on Pedestrian Psychological Responses in Urban Renewal--A Neuro-Architectural Study Using AIGC-fNIRS Framework
role: Project Leader, Experimental Design, and Theoretical Framework Development
description: Led project coordination and research design for constructing a strategy model based on fNIRS neuroimaging data; responsible for experimental planning, data processing, and statistical analysis.

researchTopic: A Systematic Review of Multimodal Large Language Models and Human Behavioral Interaction Mechanisms
organization: A Systematic Review of Multimodal Large Language Models and Human Behavioral Interaction Mechanisms
description: Conducted a systematic review of research published over the past five years on multimodal large language models (LLMs) and their interaction with human behavioral mechanisms. Constructed a structured literature screening and classification framework, and developed an auxiliary literature analysis tool to support efficient synthesis and structured aggregation. Contributed to the overall theoretical architecture of the paper and was responsible for designing the conceptual framework diagram, clarifying the logical relationships between multimodal model capability dimensions and behavioral impact mechanisms
